title: Dynamics: Newton Second Law
objective: Apply a constant force to a particle of mass 2.0. Set particle.vx to increase by 3.0 per frame (F=ma, a=F/m=6/2=3).

title: Kinetic Energy
objective: Compute the kinetic energy of a particle with mass 2.0 and velocity vx=3.0, vy=4.0. Store the result (0.5*m*v^2) in particle.x.

title: Potential Energy
objective: Compute gravitational potential energy. Given mass=2.0, gravity=9.81, and height=particle.y=10, store m*g*h in particle.x.

title: Energy Conservation
objective: Verify total energy conservation. A particle falls from y=100 with vx=0. After 20 frames, compute KE+PE and store in particle.x. Total should remain constant.

title: Oscillations and Waves
objective: Make the particle oscillate horizontally using sine. Set particle.x = 50 * sin(time) where time is the frame counter. After 15 frames, particle.x should be near 0.

title: Wave Frequency and Amplitude
objective: Control wave parameters. Set particle.y = amplitude * sin(2 * pi * frequency * t) with amplitude=30 and frequency=0.1. After 10 frames, particle.y should be near 0.

title: Electricity: Ohm Law
objective: Apply Ohm's law V=I*R. Given voltage=12 and resistance=4, compute current I=V/R and store in particle.x. Then compute power P=V*I and store in particle.y.

title: Magnetism: Lorentz Force
objective: Apply Lorentz force F=q*v*B on a charged particle. Given q=1, v=particle.vx=5, B=2, compute force and update particle.vy accordingly.

title: Thermodynamics: Temperature and Heat
objective: Model temperature change via heat transfer. Given mass=2, specific_heat=1, heat_input=100, compute delta_T = Q/(m*c) and store in particle.x.

title: Thermodynamics: Entropy
objective: Compute entropy change. Given Q=100 and T=300, compute delta_S = Q/T and store in particle.x.

title: Basic Kinematics
objective: Describe motion over time using position, velocity, and acceleration.
